action.reasoning: Observation: The ensemble of best.pt (2560x6) and resmlp.pt (2048x5) achieved a new best mean_r2 of 0.5271. The weakest group remains ptend_q (0.3248). Analysis: Adding a third diverse, high-capacity model should further reduce variance and may improve humidity tendency predictions. A wider residual MLP (3072 hidden, 6 blocks, ~114M params) with slightly stronger regularization (dropout 0.15) provides a different inductive bias. I also need a way to evaluate new models individually before ensembling them, so I will update predict.py to load either a single checkpoint (the one passed by the eval harness) or a list from an optional ensemble_config.json. Decision: Write the updated predict.py, then train the new 3072x6 model from scratch for 15 epochs and save it as checkpoints/model_c.pt. Estimated training time is ~35-40 minutes, well within the remaining ~2-hour budget.
